name: Law
description: Legal expertise is essential for building governance frameworks, liability structures, and regulatory approaches for AI systems.

people.name: Jonathan Zittrain
people.description: Designs frameworks for legal alignment to ensure AI systems are publicly accountable and ethical.

people.name: Anka Reuel
people.description: Works on AI policy and governance, developing legal frameworks for responsible AI development and deployment.

people.name: Jonas Schuett
people.description: Studies legal and regulatory approaches to AI governance, including corporate governance of AI labs and international AI law.

trajectory: Expertise in regulatory design is critical for creating effective AI governance that actually constrains dangerous development.
International law background helps navigate cross-border AI governance challenges and treaty design.
Liability and tort law knowledge informs who should be responsible when AI systems cause harm.
Tech law and IP experience helps address questions around AI model ownership, training data rights, and deployment restrictions.

projects.title: Draft model AI safety legislation
projects.description: Write sample legislative language for AI safety requirements, drawing on precedents from other regulated industries.

projects.title: Analyze AI liability frameworks
projects.description: Compare different legal theories of liability for AI harms and assess which best incentivize safety investment.

projects.title: International AI governance treaty analysis
projects.description: Study existing international technology agreements and propose frameworks for international AI safety cooperation.
